response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and backend web developer whose profile is fundamentally misaligned with this C++-centric industrial automation role. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2015 and a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not appear in his listed skills at all. The role demands deep C++ and STL expertise, industrial cybersecurity knowledge per IEC 62443, MQTT and industrial protocol experience (OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850), and IoT/embedded systems work — none of which are substantively represented in his 15-year career, which has been oriented entirely toward web, SaaS, and open-source geo-data platforms.
strengths: Extensive full-stack and backend engineering experience (15+ years) with strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development
Demonstrated event-driven and distributed systems design through microservices work at Dunnhumby and the GISCollective geo-data platform
High-throughput, low-latency backend systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, showing a performance-oriented engineering mindset transferable in principle
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated — roughly six months in 2015 at National Instruments plus a thesis project; C++ is absent from the skills section entirely
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, IEC 62443 SDL practices, or industrial protocols such as MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, or IEC 61850
No IoT, embedded systems, or industrial automation domain background beyond a peripheral NI LabWindows stint; entire career trajectory is web and SaaS

key_factors.factor: Strong distributed systems and software craftsmanship
